info.title: FreeUnit - Community LTS Fork of Unit Application Server
info.description: FreeUnit is a community-driven continuation of the Unit application server, originally developed by Nginx/F5. The original repository was archived in October 2025. FreeUnit provides ongoing security updates, PHP 8.4/8.5 support, and OpenTelemetry integration.

main_sections.name: Introduction
main_sections.description: Main description of FreeUnit as a community fork of Unit application server

main_sections.name: Why FreeUnit
main_sections.description: Key reasons for the fork: PHP 8.4/8.5 support, security backports, stable LTS releases, community governance

main_sections.name: Installation
main_sections.description: Build from source instructions. Official packages and Docker images coming soon.

main_sections.name: OpenTelemetry Integration
main_sections.description: Native OpenTelemetry support for tracing and telemetry collection

main_sections.name: Project Status
main_sections.description: Current status: active development, GitHub org created, PHP 8.5 working, Docker images in progress

main_sections.name: Community
main_sections.description: Links to GitHub discussions, issues, contact email, contributing guide

main_sections.name: Documentation
main_sections.description: Full documentation site for FreeUnit — installation, configuration, API reference

main_sections.name: Telegram Community
main_sections.description: Official FreeUnit Telegram group for community chat and announcements

key_concepts.definition: Community LTS fork of the Unit application server, created after Nginx/F5 archived the original repository in October 2025.
A dynamic application server that runs applications in multiple languages (PHP, Python, Go, JavaScript, Ruby, Java) with unified configuration.
The daemon process of Unit/FreeUnit application server.
Native integration in FreeUnit for collecting traces and telemetry data from applications.

differences: FreeUnit continues active development (original was archived in Oct 2025)
Unit/FreeUnit is an application server, not primarily a web server
Dynamic language runtimes (PHP, Python, etc.) are built-in
No need for separate PHP-FPM or uWSGI
Configuration is dynamic via API, no reload needed
Single process model vs Apache's prefork/worker
Lower memory footprint for many concurrent connections

faq.question: Is FreeUnit production ready?
faq.answer: The project is active and building successfully with PHP 8.5 support. Official packages and Docker images are in progress. For production use, consider building from source or wait for official releases.

faq.question: Why was FreeUnit created?
faq.answer: The original Unit repository was archived by Nginx/F5 in October 2025 with a note that 'A new maintainer is desired.' FreeUnit is the community's response to continue active development, especially for PHP 8.4/8.5 support.

faq.question: How do I install FreeUnit?
faq.answer: Currently, build from source: git clone https://github.com/freeunitorg/freeunit, then ./configure --openssl --otel, make, and sudo make unitd-install. Official packages coming soon.

faq.question: Does FreeUnit support Docker?
faq.answer: Docker images are 'in progress' according to the project status. You can build your own image using the source code and Dockerfile template.

faq.question: What programming languages does FreeUnit support?
faq.answer: Same as original Unit: PHP (including 8.4/8.5), Python, Go, JavaScript/Node.js, Ruby, Java, Perl. Additional language modules can be built as needed.

faq.question: Is FreeUnit compatible with original Unit configuration?
faq.answer: Yes, FreeUnit maintains API compatibility with the original Unit, so existing configurations should work without changes.

ai_instructions.summary_prompt: FreeUnit is a community-maintained fork of the Unit application server, created after Nginx/F5 archived the original. It provides ongoing security updates, PHP 8.4/8.5 support, and OpenTelemetry integration. Build from source currently; official packages coming soon.
common_use_cases: Running PHP applications with modern PHP 8.4/8.5
